task_title: Audit document processing pipeline (document_processor.py, reranking.py, query_transformer.py, utils.py)
false_positives_removed: datapipeline-001 — shape.has_table guarantees shape.table in python-pptx; not a real issue

findings.title: Silent fallback to pypdf masks missing pdfplumber dependency
findings.problem: When pdfplumber is unavailable, ImportError is caught and pypdf is used as fallback. The pypdf fallback uses identical iteration structure but different extraction quality. Users get no warning that they're running with degraded PDF extraction. The fallback was likely written during initial prototyping and never removed after pdfplumber became the intended dependency.
findings.fix: Either remove the pypdf fallback (fail with ImportError if pdfplumber missing), or log a WARNING when falling back so users know extraction quality is degraded.
findings.ai_pattern: Context rot: pypdf fallback likely leftover from initial prototyping

findings.title: Function parameter shadowed by local reassignment
findings.problem: The parameter `directory` (str) is reassigned to `Path(directory)`, shadowing the original string value. If any code between the function entry and line 234 references the original string (e.g., in error messages), the original is lost. This also confuses type checkers and IDEs.
findings.fix: Use a different variable name: `dir_path = Path(directory)`

findings.title: CrossEncoder.predict() return value not validated before use
findings.problem: The model.predict() return value is assumed to be a properly sized list/array. If the model fails to load, returns unexpected shape, or the input pairs cause an error, downstream sorting will fail with confusing errors instead of a clear validation message.
findings.fix: Add validation after predict(): verify return is list/ndarray and length matches input chunks count.
findings.evidence:
scores = self.model.predict(sentence_pairs)
results = [(chunk, score) for chunk, score in zip(chunks, scores)]

findings.title: Duplicate scoring/sorting logic in rerank() and rerank_with_scores()
findings.problem: Both rerank() (lines 51-58) and rerank_with_scores() (lines 78-85) contain identical code for creating sentence pairs, calling model.predict, zipping with scores, and sorting by score descending. This violates DRY and means bug fixes must be applied in two places.
findings.fix: Extract shared logic into a private method: _score_and_sort(query, chunks) -> List[Tuple[DocumentChunk, float]]
findings.evidence:
sentence_pairs = [(query, chunk.text) for chunk in chunks]
scores = self.model.predict(sentence_pairs)
results = [(chunk, score) for chunk, score in zip(chunks, scores)]
results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

findings.title: chunk_text is a god function with multiple responsibilities
findings.problem: The chunk_text method (~63 lines) handles paragraph splitting, sentence tokenization, chunk building, overlap handling, and oversized sentence edge cases. This makes it difficult to test individual behaviors and increases cognitive load.

findings.title: Import inside method body instead of module level
findings.problem: InferenceConfig is imported inside transform_step_back() on every call. Module-level import would fail-fast at load time and avoid repeated import resolution overhead.
findings.fix: Move `from llm_interface import InferenceConfig` to the top of the file with other imports.
findings.ai_pattern: Context rot: likely leftover from refactoring

findings.title: Stop words set recreated on every call
findings.problem: The stop_words frozenset is reconstructed every time transform_keywords() is called. Since this is an immutable constant, it should be defined once at class or module level.
findings.fix: Define as class constant: STOP_WORDS = frozenset({'a', 'an', 'the', 'is', ...})

findings.title: Encoding fallback order suboptimal for common cases
findings.problem: The encoding fallback list ['utf-8', 'utf-16', 'latin-1', 'cp1252'] tries utf-16 before latin-1. UTF-16 files are rare in practice; latin-1 and cp1252 (Windows Western European) are far more common for legacy documents. Suboptimal order means common cases require trying and failing utf-16 first.

findings.title: Silent exception swallowing in process_file hides errors from callers
findings.problem: Any exception during file processing is caught, printed to stdout, and returns an empty list. Callers cannot distinguish between 'no chunks extracted' (legitimate) and 'processing crashed' (error). This masks data loss.
findings.fix: Re-raise as a custom DocumentProcessingError, or return a result object with success/failure status and error details.

findings.title: Magic numbers for chunk_size and chunk_overlap hardcoded
findings.problem: Default values of 256 (chunk_size) and 50 (chunk_overlap) are hardcoded in the constructor. These tuning parameters should come from configuration to allow experimentation without code changes.

findings.title: Missing return type hint on transform_step_back
findings.problem: The public method transform_step_back() lacks a return type annotation, reducing API clarity and preventing static type checking.
findings.fix: Add return type: def transform_step_back(self, query: str) -> str:

findings.title: Missing return type hint on transform_keywords
findings.problem: The public method transform_keywords() lacks a return type annotation.
findings.fix: Add return type: def transform_keywords(self, query: str) -> str:

findings.title: Missing type hint on __init__ llm parameter
findings.problem: The llm parameter in QueryTransformer.__init__ lacks a type annotation.
findings.fix: Add type: def __init__(self, llm: Any) -> None:

findings.title: No validation for empty text input in chunk_text
findings.problem: If the input text is empty or whitespace-only, chunk_text silently returns an empty list without logging. This may hide data quality issues where a document is expected to have content but produces none.
findings.fix: Add early-return with a WARNING log if text is empty/whitespace.

findings.title: No binary file detection before text extraction
findings.problem: extract_text_file() does not check whether a file is binary before attempting text extraction with encoding fallback. Binary files will be read with errors='replace' producing garbage chunks that pollute the vector store.
findings.fix: Add binary detection before extraction: check first 8KB for null bytes or high ratio of non-printable characters.

findings.title: Missing module docstring
findings.problem: utils.py lacks a module-level docstring. While only 23 lines, it provides the core RRF fusion function and should be documented.
findings.fix: Reciprocal Rank Fusion and utility functions for the RAG pipeline.

findings.title: CrossEncoder model name hardcoded instead of configurable
findings.problem: The default model_name for the CrossEncoder is hardcoded. Model selection should be configurable via settings to allow swapping without code changes.
findings.fix: Read from settings: model_name=settings.cross_encoder_model or pass as config parameter
